title: Held-out per-decision eval (HUD gateway)
what: Each model answers the SAME frozen held-out scenarios independently (not games, no opponent) and is scored by a deterministic reward function vs ground-truth. Held-out = the grader_games boards, disjoint from training. Both models forced /no_think for a fair pick-quality comparison.
vs_headtohead: This is per-decision 'exam' scoring; the head-to-head matchups (other section) are full games, model-vs-base, scored by who wins.

envs.note: Clear generalization to unseen boards; placement-only and full chain are identical (no forgetting).
Builds instead of hoarding — clear generalization (0.93→1.38 reward, 78→97% build).
Trained model stopped over-trading (21%→3%) — our #1 discovered failure mode — but reward gain is marginal (over-corrected; skips some good trades).
